California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
Mark Fogelman, counsel
Pubfic UtiLities commission
505 van Ness Avenue
San Francisco, cA 94102-3294
January l-1, 1991
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-618
Dear Dtr. Fogelman:
This is. in response to your request for advice regarding the
confl ict-of-interesl 
and other provisions of the Political 
Reform
Act (the 'Act").1 
Because your request is in the nature of
g"""i"i 
guidance, we are treating your request as one for. informaL
i=.i"t.rr6" 
pursuant to negulatioi iazaglcf lcopy-enclosed) '2 
The
ia"i"" is lirnited to your duties and responsibil.ities under the
Act since we cannot provide third-party advice'
OUESTION
If the California Public Utilities 
Conurission (cPUc)
reirnburses you for the cost of aj-r transPortation and- hotel
accommodati6ns for your parti'cipation as a panelist at a
"o.rf".".t"" in washington, O.C., and the CPUC is, in turn,
reimbursed by the coiporation holding the conference, will the
"".p"titi""'i 
reirnburiement constitute a gift or income to you?
1 co.r".n*"nt Code Section BlOOO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to tlre Government Code unl-ess otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 cal'ifornia code of Regulations Section
18600, et seq-.- AIl references to regulations are to Title 2,
oivisions 6 of tne California code of Regulations'
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
lcovernine-nt code Seation gltla; 2 cal- Code of Regs. Section
1823e (c) (3) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322-5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-6■8
Page 2
CONCLUSION
Based on the facts provided' the reimbursement by the world
Expo Corporation, the corporation holding the conference, to the
cPUc will not constitute a gi,ft or income to you.
FACTS
The world Expo corporation, a subsidiary of International
Data Group (IDG), has invj,ted the california Public Utilities
Commission (CPUC) to provide a panelist at the Comnet '91-
Conference scheduled for January 29, L991,, concerning the lega]
implications and developments likely to occur in the
telecommunications industry. 
You are a designated employee of the
CPUC and may be the Legal Division representative of the cPUc
assigned to participate in the panel. The corporation has offered
to r6imburse the cPUc for the cost of the participant/s round-trip
coach non-stop air fare from Cal-ifornia to washington, D'c', and
for one night-'s hotel rate (government or conference rate, if
possible) . The cPUc representative wiLl be reimbursed by the cPUc
ior his or her expenses as out-of-state travel' on state business'
The cPUc wilI, in turn, bill' the World ExPo corPoration to recover
the air fare and hotel exPenses.
ANALYSI S
The thrust of your question is whether the reirnbursenent of
the cost of air transportation and hotel expenses by the.world
Expo corporation to tle cPUc, a state agency, .wil1 constitute a
giit or income to you, giving rise to a confl ict-of-interest 
or
5ther problern undei the Act. 
rncome or gifts valued.at $250 or
more r-eceived by a public officiat during the preceding 12 months
may require aisqua f if ication from any decision which will have a
relsonibly foreseeable material financial' effect on the donors
which is distinquisnable frorn the effect on the public generally'
(Section 87103. )
Income
You are a publi,c official 
of a state agency as defined in
Section 82048. In your letter you have stated that if you are
selected to be the agency representative, you will be reinbursed
by the cPUc for your travel expenses as a normal out-of-state
tiavel on state business. Reirnbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state agency is excluded from reporting as incone
under Section 82030(b) (2). Therefore, such reirnbursement would
not constitute incorne to you, giving rise to a conflict-of-
interest or reporting obligation under the Act.


Direct Reimbursement
The result may differ if the corporation reimburses you
directly for your travel expenses. In such case, the
reimbur-sement of travel expenses may be a reportable gift or
income to you. we have advised that reimbursernents for travel and
expenses by private parties to officials are gifts to the
of-f icial,s wnen traveling on official, busj.ness unl-ess the provision
of travel expenses rneets specified criteria set forth in In re
Stone (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 52 (copy enclosed). (Also, see Kol-odnev
aarice Letter, No. A-87-182 and Knox Advice Letter, No. A-90-284,
copies also enclosed. )
Please note that beginning January L, ]-99Lt gifts to
designated employees3 valued at more than S25o from any single
sour6" in a cilenaar year are prohibited if the ernployee is
required to report the receipt of. income or gifts from that source
on hj.s or her ltaternent of economic interest. 
(section 89505')
Gifts of $so or more continue to be rePortable on financial
disclosure staternents. (section 87207(a) (1).) 
since your travel
expenses are not rePortable income or gifts under the facts
prlsented, it is not necessary to apPly section 89505' The new
iaw also provides that travel which is provided by the agency of
an elected state officer, 
member of a state board or commission,
or a designated employee is not a gift subject to the $250 linit'
(section 8es06 (d) (2) . )
fn summary, you have indicated that you are a desS-gnated
employee of a state agency. If you are selected to be the agency
relrelentative you wil-I be reimbursed by the cPUc for your travel
exp"nses to a conference in Washington D.c. on January 28 and 29,
1901. Travel provided to you by the agency' therefore, is not
reportable incone or gift to you provided the travel is treated as
a -norrnal out-of-state travel on state business.
I trust this letter has provided you with guidance you
requested. I apologize for not respondingT to you earLier. If you
have any further questions reqarding this natter, please contact
ne at (916) 322-59oL.
Our Fi■e No. ■-90-6■8
Page 3
SH : LM: aA
Enc losures
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel
'' 
.1,
,4 ur, "-- //8''t c'L t c'--
By: Luisa llenchaca
Counsel, Lega1 Division
The Act defines 'rdesignated employee" in Section 82019
